issue_title: Docker auth: IllegalArgumentException on multiple resource scopes
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

authorization-services

### Describe the bug

I am using keycloak with the docker auth feature as authentication for a docker registry (official registry image). I am building and pushing a new image to this registry which has a base image also located in this registry with kaniko.

This fails with following error:
```
2023-02-19 22:11:57,326 WARN  [org.keycloak.services] (executor-thread-0) KC-SERVICES0013: Failed authentication: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Expecting input string to have 3 parts delineated by a ':' character.  Found 5 parts: repository:my-image:pull,push repository:my-base-image:pull
        at org.keycloak.representations.docker.DockerAccess.<init>(DockerAccess.java:47)
        at org.keycloak.protocol.docker.mapper.AllowAllDockerProtocolMapper.transformDockerResponseToken(AllowAllDockerProtocolMapper.java:46)
        at org.keycloak.protocol.docker.DockerAuthV2Protocol.lambda$authenticated$2(DockerAuthV2Protocol.java:117)
```
This is because the parser of the resource scope is only expecting a single resource scope but kaniko sends multiple (one with pull,push for the image being built and one with pull for the base image in the FROM clause in the Dockerfile). This is valid according to the docker spec: https://docs.docker.com/registry/spec/auth/scope/#authorization-server-use

"The POST request only takes in a single scope field but **may use a space to separate a list of multiple resource scopes**."



### Version

20.0.3

### Expected behavior

There is no error: the authentication succeeds and the image is allowed to be pushed to the registry.

The docker auth protocol follows the docker spec.

commit_message:

issue_title: Duplicated set-cookie headers sent causing issues with proxies
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

I'm using the keycloak-js (20.0.3)  plugin for javascript and when I upgrade the keycloak to version 20.0.2 or 20.0.3 fails with a 502 after session expired or deleted manually. 
In the brokens keycloak versions I need to every time clear the cookies in the edge browser. 
When I clear the cookies it works again.
The version 20.0.1 it works well when the session expired the cookies are removed automatically KEYCLOAK_SESSION, etc.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/26796175/220076361-db11b525-3984-4c5d-828e-128c89c0c1ae.png)


### Version

20.0.3

### Expected behavior

When the session is expired or deleted for a user in a realm, it shows the login page without error (for example in version 20.0.1)

### Actual behavior

When the session is expired or deleted for a user in a realm it shows a 502 error until manually clear the cookies KEYCLOAK_SESSION, etc.  the login page with an error 502  (for example in version 20.0.3)

### How to Reproduce?

Use de npm package keycloak-js (20.0.3)  in a web-app try to login in a keycloak-20.0.3 it will not work well after the session is deleted or expired. (But if you clear the cookies or incgonite page yes) In version 20.0.1 it works well without doing nothing.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Remove duplicated set-cookie header from response when expiring cookies

Closes #17192

issue_title: Link "Sign out" incorrectly hardcoded to localhost in the authz example applications
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

testsuite

### Describe the bug

There is a regression in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/496c6d59 .In the `authUri`, the host is hardcoded to `localhost`. This is fine in the `hello-world-authz-service` where the host was hardcoded even before this change.

However it is not fine in `servlet-authz` and `servlet-policy-enforcer` where the host was not hardcoded to `localhost` before this change.

### Version

20.0.3
commit_message:
Link 'Sign out' incorrectly hardcoded to localhost in the authz example applications
closes #17216

issue_title: the InfoPage after an ExecuteActionsEmail is not localized based on the user's locale
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

token-exchange

### Describe the bug

In my case an execute-actions-email is sent to a user for them to update their password. After the user clicks on the link in the execute-action-email they will visit an infopage listing the actions to execute followed by the update-password-page. The infopage is translated according to the locale accepted by the browser-request but update-password-page is translated according to the locale set in the user's profile.

If these two locales are not equal the translation changes during the workflow.

### Version

20.0.5

### Expected behavior

The first page should be translated by the user's locale aswell.

### Actual behavior

The first page and the second page are translated differently if the browser-requested locale and the user-profile's locale are not equal. 

### How to Reproduce?

precondition:
- a realm with a working smtp config and two supported languages (for example: german and english)
- a browser without a cookie specifying a locale for keycloak that is set to one of the supported languages (for example: german)

1. create a user with a specific locale (for example: english)
2. trigger an execute-actions-email for the UPDATE_PASSWORD action
3. the email is in english
4. open the link send by mail in the browser
5. the infopage will open in german, the language accepted by the browser
6. the following update-password-page will open in englisch, the use-profile's locale



### Anything else?

I might have already identified the underlying issue: the authenticed user is not loaded into the loginformsprovider in ExecuteActionsActionTokenHandler::handleToken.

I plan to provide a pull-request shortly.
commit_message:
setting the user in ExecuteActionsActionTokenHandler.handleToken to manage user null case in FreeMarkerLoginFormsProvider.createResponse

Closes #17233

Signed-off-by: Rishabh Singh <rishabhsvats@gmail.com>

issue_title: admin-ui: authz viewing of a group requires also view-members permission
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When using fine-grained permissions on a group and only having the view permission, I am unable to open a group in the admin ui.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1793007/220675748-71f1ac93-a518-4e25-b968-2f9534b307f9.png)

### Version

20.0.3

### Environment

_No response_

### Expected behavior

The group is opened and the members tab is hidden, as the user has no permissions do view or manage the members of the group.

### Actual behavior

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1793007/220675748-71f1ac93-a518-4e25-b968-2f9534b307f9.png)

Request failed with status code 403

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a realm `testrealm`
2. Create a user `testuser` with `query-groups` role
3. Add credentials for that user
4. Create a group `A` and enable fine grained permissions for that group
5. In `clients` -> `realm-management` -> `authorization` -> `policies` create a user policy for the `testuser`
6. Assign that policy to the view permission of the group.
7. Log in to the admin console of the `testrealm` with the created `testuser`
8. Open `Groups`
9. Click on group `A`

### Anything else?

Debugging the network traffic reveals that the group details are returned, but then the UI proceeds to request the members of the group which then returns a `403` error. The members should not be requested if the user does not have the permissions to do so (view-/manage-members).
commit_message:
Add access to full group tree.  Fix access for members tab. Add missing (#19423)

props to Access object.
Fixes #17589

issue_title: Typo in Outgoing HTTP requests documentation
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

docs

### Describe the bug

The documentation [outgoinghttp.adoc](docs/guides/src/main/server/outgoinghttp.adoc) gives an example Proxy mapping command line `*\.(google|googleapis)\.com` which is incorrect. It should be `.*\.(google|googleapis)\.com` with a dot at the beginning.

Without the `.`, keycloak prints an error message when issuing a outgoing request and the proxy configuration is not taken into account.

### Version

20.0.5

### Expected behavior

This is a typo in the documentation only.

### Actual behavior

This is a typo in the documentation only.

### How to Reproduce?

This is a typo in the documentation only.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Typo in Outgoing HTTP requests documentation

closes #17242

commit_message:
Fix NPE in MigrateTo21_0_0 when admin theme is not set explicitly (#17249)

Only update admin-console theme to keycloak.v2 if it is explicitly set to "keycloak" or "rh-sso".

Fixes #17248

Signed-off-by: Thomas Darimont <thomas.darimont@googlemail.com>
Co-authored-by: Stian Thorgersen <stian@redhat.com>

### Expected behavior

The Keycloak server does not get internal server errors when the user attempts to login multiple times in a row

### Actual behavior

Refreshing/requesting the token of the user does end up in a 500 internal server error response

Use a frontend library to initiate the OIDC login automatically when you open the frontend website.
Refresh the website in your browser in a quick succession after the login was successful using the same session (do not use private windows or clear cookies in-between). 
After some attempts, the abovementioned exception will be thrown. 

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: New Referrer-policy breaks cross-origin SP<->IdP (KC)
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

saml

### Describe the bug

Facing same issue as  [12008](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/12008#issue-1237144963) in latest releases
Sometimes some Service Providers (SAML) hosted on a specific domain implements CORS policy directive to prevent illegal cross-domain acess. When Keycloak is used as an Identity Provider (SAML) to secure access of these Service Providers with POST-binding profile it fails because of default Referrer-Policy set to 'no-referrer' from 11.0.1 Keycloak Version.

Here is a sample

User-Agent -> Service Provider A (a.com) : User wants to access Service Provider A
User-Agent <- Service Provider A (a.com) : Service Provider returns an html resource with form containing SAMLRequest and the action pointing to the Identity Provider B (b.com)
User-Agent -> Identity Provider B (b.com) : User-Agent POSTs (when page is loaded) SAMLRequest form
User-Agent <- Identity Provider B (b.com) : ... After user authentication ... Identity Provider B returns an html with form containing SAMLResponse and the action pointing to the Service Provider A (a.com) + an http header 'Referrer-Policy' with no-referrer' policy set.
User-Agent -> User-Agent : As Identity Provider B tells to the User-Agent it doesn't want the Referer header (and moreover Origin) be valued (no-referrer policy definition), User-Agent remove http header Referer and set Origin http header to null before POSTing SAMLResponse form
User-Agent -> Service Provider A (a.com) : User agent POSTs (when page is loaded) SAMLResponse form. Origin is at this time null.
Service Provider A (a.com) -> Service Provider A (a.com) : The Service Provider A detects a cross-origin invocation (coming from b.com) and check Origin (which is null). null being not permitted, the Service Provider stops the request and returns to the User-Agent an 'invalid CORS request' error response.

### Version

20.0.5 , 21.0.0

### Expected behavior

Referrer-Policy shoud be computed considering the different use case : If the SP has a cross-origin (can be obtain through the ACS URL) we should override the default policy when returning saml-post-form resource to accept cross-origin invocation.



### Actual behavior

Due to Referrer-Policy set to no-referrer, the User-Agent send html form without Referer header nor Origin (which is set to null).



### How to Reproduce?

Create a SP with a domain (a.com) and an IdP with a domain (b.com)
Implements CORS Policy on SP domain
Register SP on IdP
Triggers a 'login' flow.

### Anything else?

Default policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin should be more appropriate.

issue_title: javax.net.ssl.SSLException exceptions because org.keycloak.adapters.HttpClientBuilder ignores connectionTTL setting
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

The property connectionTTL can be set but it is never used in the org.keycloak.adapters.HttpClientBuilder#build method. This results that connections live forever, resulting in Connection reset Exceptions, when the connection was closed on the server.

The PoolingHttpClientConnectionManager should be created like this:

```
PoolingHttpClientConnectionManager tcm = new PoolingHttpClientConnectionManager(sf.build(), null, null, null, connectionTTL, connectionTTLUnit);
```

If the poolsize is set to 1 its more difficult. Could be fixed by setting the keepAliveStrategy on BasicHttpClientConnectionManager

### Version

20.0.1

### Expected behavior

Property connectionTTL  of org.keycloak.adapters.HttpClientBuilder should be used when creating a httpClient

### Actual behavior

Property connectionTTL  of org.keycloak.adapters.HttpClientBuilder is ignored when creating a httpClient

### How to Reproduce?

- Use a code like this to create an httpClient:
```
            HttpClientBuilder httpClientBuilder = new HttpClientBuilder();

            LOGGER.info("Setting timeouts socketTimeoutSeconds={}, connectionTTLSeconds={}, establishConnectionTimeoutSeconds={}",
                    adapterConfig.getSocketTimeoutSeconds(), adapterConfig.getConnectionTtlSeconds(), adapterConfig.getEstablishConnectionTimeoutSeconds());
            httpClientBuilder.socketTimeout(adapterConfig.getSocketTimeoutSeconds(), TimeUnit.SECONDS);
            httpClientBuilder.connectionTTL(adapterConfig.getConnectionTtlSeconds(), TimeUnit.SECONDS);
            httpClientBuilder.establishConnectionTimeout(adapterConfig.getEstablishConnectionTimeoutSeconds(), TimeUnit.SECONDS);
            HttpClient client = httpClientBuilder.build(adapterConfig);
```

- Use this client to fetch tokens from keycloak. 
- Have some component in between that closes idle connection. This happens for example when an AWS NAT Gateway is in between, that closes the connection after  350 seconds with an RST packet
- Try to fetch the token again

=> javax.net.ssl.SSLException: Connection reset

### Anything else?
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/49994901/connection-reset-by-keycloaks-java-adapter
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/nat-gateway-troubleshooting.html
commit_message:
Set connection TTL in adapters HttpClientBuilder
closes #17304

issue_title: Disabling features with disabled dependencies fails "Feature account2 depends on disabled feature account-api"
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

I'm building Keycloak as part of a docker container build and the below command fails:
bin/kc.sh build \
--http-relative-path=/auth \
--features=admin-api,admin-fine-grained-authz,admin2,js-adapter \
--features-disabled=**account-api,account2**,authorization,ciba,client-policies,client-secret-rotation,declarative-user-profile,docker,dynamic-scopes,fips,impersonation,kerberos,map-storage,openshift-integration,par,preview,recovery-codes,scripts,step-up-authentication,token-exchange,update-email,web-authn

with:
```
2023-02-28 15:27:17,657 TRACE [io.quarkus.bootstrap.classloading.QuarkusClassLoader] (main) Class quarkus.properties not found in parent first load from java.net.URLClassLoader@6aeb35e6

2023-02-28 15:27:17,657 TRACE [io.quarkus.bootstrap.classloading.QuarkusClassLoader] (main) Class quarkus.properties not found in parent first load from java.net.URLClassLoader@6aeb35e6

ERROR: Failed to run 'build' command.
ERROR: Feature account2 depends on disabled feature account-api
ERROR: Feature account2 depends on disabled feature account-api
For more details run the same command passing the '--verbose' option. Also you can use '--help' to see the details about the usage of the particular command.
```

Both features in the error messages are in the --features-disabled argument (as highlighted).

I traced the code to the keycloak-common project in the class org.keycloak.common.Profile and it seams the below check to make sure dependencies are correct is failing.
```
    private static void verifyConfig(Map<Feature, Boolean> features) {
        for (Feature f : features.keySet()) {
            if (f.getDependencies() != null) {
                for (Feature d : f.getDependencies()) {
                    if (!features.get(d)) {
                        throw new ProfileException("Feature " + f.getKey() + " depends on disabled feature " + d.getKey());
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }
```
The if statement on the 3rd line should check if the feature itself is enabled before continuing with the checks.  


On a side note, the message in the error output to use --verbose should be removed.  add --verbose to the command outputs 
`Unknown option: '--verbose'`

### Version

21.0.0

### Expected behavior

[keycloak-success-output.txt](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/files/10847001/keycloak-success-output.txt)


### Actual behavior

[keycloak-failed-output.txt](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/files/10846992/keycloak-failed-output.txt)


### How to Reproduce?

execute the following:

bin/kc.sh build \
--http-relative-path=/auth \
--features=admin-api,admin-fine-grained-authz,admin2,js-adapter \
--features-disabled=account-api,account2,authorization,ciba,client-policies,client-secret-rotation,declarative-user-profile,docker,dynamic-scopes,fips,impersonation,kerberos,map-storage,openshift-integration,par,preview,recovery-codes,scripts,step-up-authentication,token-exchange,update-email,web-authn

### Anything else?

issue_title: Connection string for ldap user federation with multiple hosts no longer supported
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

Since 21.0.0 the connection string "ldaps://ldap-server-1 ldaps://ldap-server-2" is not longer supported.


### Version

21.0.0

### Expected behavior

The connection string is still valid and will be used for failover scenarios.
When the first host is down the second one will be used

### Actual behavior

Connection string is not valid
Migration guide from 20.x does not contain a note for migrating this connection string

### How to Reproduce?

Try to use the ldap connection string "ldaps://ldap-server-1 ldaps://ldap-server-2"

### Anything else?

When the first host is down the second will be used.
If there is another way to provide this kind of "failover" feature I would use it.

Another way could be to "clone" oder "duplicate" the ldap user fedaration but there is no function for this. So all mappers must be entered twice.

Are there any plans to readd the support for failover ldap connection strings?
commit_message:
Fix the issue with LDAP connectionUrl containing multiple hosts
Closes #17359

issue_title: User session limit make account console crash and logout the user
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

After setting the user session limits to 1 per realm and with terminate oldest session behavior, the account console crashes after a simple page refresh showing "failed to initialize keycloak" and logging out the user. In the server logs it emits the following warning message:

` type=CODE_TO_TOKEN_ERROR, realmId=myrealm, clientId=account-console, userId=null, ipAddress=172.19.0.1, error=invalid_code, grant_type=authorization_code, code_id=4103ed42-c407-476e-ae58-9860645c7d74, client_auth_method=client-secret`

If i bind again the browser flow to the built-in flow, it works.

### Version

21.0.0

### Expected behavior

The refresh doesn't cause any logout / crash.

### Actual behavior

The user is logged out and the account console app crashes.

### How to Reproduce?

Setup the user session limits by following the [documentation](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/index.html#_user_session_limits). This means that:
- The limiter has to be configured with all the required flows
- Alternative steps/subflows must be wrapped into subflows to avoid ALTERNATIVE - REQUIRED at the same level

To check if I misconfigured / wrapped the flows, I provide the JSON of the imported realm at: [https://pastebin.com/uASeP5bz](https://pastebin.com/uASeP5bz)

### Anything else?

I had the same bug also with version 20 and 19.
commit_message:
Clarify user session limits documentation and test SSO scenario (#19372)

Closes #17374

issue_title: Keycloak 21.0.1 - Paging and filtering not working in "Assign roles" popup"
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

In the new Admin UI, in "Role Mapping" tab for a user, when clicking on "Assign roles" button, the popup will display only the first page of role results. Clicking on next, will display the same first page of results, so its not possible to display subsequent pages.

When entering search term in "Search by role name" field, the search term doesn't affect the result, but the same unfiltered first page of results is displayed.

The requests sent to keycloak server: 
- /role-mappings/realm/available?first=**0**&max=11&search=ROLE_USER - result contains first 51 roles (I have 60 in the realm), but the UI displays only first 10
- /role-mappings/realm/available?first=**20**&max=11&search=ROLE_USER - result contains first 51 roles (I have 60 in the realm), but the UI displays only first 10, so the "first" and "search" parameter are not taken into account at all.

Changing page size to 100, will display all 51 roles that were loaded, but that's not complete list of roles. If there are more then 51 roles int he realm, the remaining roles above 51 are not displayed.

### Version

21.0.1

### Expected behavior

- Clicking on next should display next page instead of first page only
- Entering search term should display only matching roles

### Actual behavior

- Clicking on next shows first page only
- Entering search term has no effect on search results (non-matching roles are retrieved)

### How to Reproduce?

- Add more then 10 roles to the realm
- See that paging and filtering on "Assign roles" popup don't work

### Anything else?

This is pretty much a blocker for us for upgrading to version 21. In version 20 the issue also exists, but then at least we had an option to switch back to old admin UI. Now that the old admin UI has been removed, there is no workaround for us any more.

issue_title: robots.txt causes indexing
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

authentication/webauthn

### Describe the bug

The keycloak subdomain of one of our projects was recently found in the google search results (while searching for the project name, not the url directly). The search result does not contain any page excerpt (not even the title), only the subdomain is displayed. 

The issue here is that the robots.txt disallows robots from crawling the contents, and then the templates contain `noindex, nofollow` to prevent indexing. However, to my understanding the `noindex, nofollow` never comes into effect because robots are forbidden from crawling the documents in the first place. It does not prevent indexing. 
```
User-agent: *
Disallow: /
```

```html
<meta name="robots" content="noindex, nofollow">
```

We would like to remove the robots.txt altogether. However, this is not a static resource but generated by [RobotsResource.java](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/RobotsResource.java). 

### Version

17

### Expected behavior

There is no robots.txt or the contents is customizable

### Actual behavior

There is a robots.txt which prevents crawling but not indexing and it is not customizable

### How to Reproduce?

Access /robots.txt on the keycloak host

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Remove robots.txt entirely

* remove robots.txt entirely, as blocking page-
crawling prevents the `X-Robots-Tag` headers
(and similar meta tags) from working as intended.

Closes #17433

Signed-off-by: Andy <andy@slice.is>
Signed-off-by: Alexander Schwartz <aschwart@redhat.com>
Co-authored-by: Alexander Schwartz <aschwart@redhat.com>
